ai-102.title: AI-102 Azure AI Engineer Practice Exam
ai-102.description: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate

topics.name: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
modules: Select the appropriate Azure AI service
Plan and configure security for an AI service
Create and manage an Azure AI service
Plan and implement networking for an Azure AI service

topics.name: Implement computer vision solutions
modules: Implement custom computer vision models by using Azure Machine Learning

topics.name: Implement natural language processing solutions
modules: Analyze text by using Azure AI Language
Process speech by using Azure AI Speech
Implement and manage a language understanding model

topics.name: Implement knowledge mining and document intelligence solutions
modules: Implement an Azure AI Search solution
Implement an Azure AI Document Intelligence solution

topics.name: Implement generative AI solutions
modules: Use Azure OpenAI Service to generate content

questions.question: You need to prevent a generative model from returning unsafe outputs while building a chat app in East US. Which capability should you enable?
options: Content filtering with Azure AI Content Safety
questions.explanation: Azure AI Content Safety provides input and output filtering for harmful content. Tuning temperature or stop sequences does not guarantee safety.

reasoning.correct: Content Safety is designed to detect and block unsafe categories, aligning with Responsible AI requirements.

why_others_wrong.0: Reduces randomness but cannot enforce safety policies.
why_others_wrong.1: Controls throughput but not safety categories.
why_others_wrong.2: Stops generation at tokens but does not classify or block harmful content.

questions.question: You are designing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for app-marketing-1001. Which approaches improve grounding and reduce hallucinations? (Select two)
options: Add citations to the prompt format and require sources
Disable system prompt to allow free-form answers
Use embeddings with a vector store for semantic search
questions.explanation: Semantic retrieval with embeddings grounds the model in enterprise data; prompting for citations improves traceability.

reasoning.correct: RAG pairs relevant context with prompts; requiring citations encourages grounded outputs and improves reviewability.

why_others_wrong.0: Temperature > 1 increases randomness and does not ground answers.
why_others_wrong.2: Removes guardrails and increases hallucination likelihood.
why_others_wrong.3: Larger context without relevant sources does not ground answers.

questions.question: Your API app in West Europe must call Azure OpenAI without storing keys in code. What should you use?
options: Hard-coded API key in environment variable
questions.explanation: Managed identity enables Azure AD-based authentication without secrets in code.

reasoning.correct: Managed identities remove the need for key management and integrate with RBAC.

why_others_wrong.0: Insecure and not supported.
why_others_wrong.1: Environment variables are still secrets to rotate and protect.
why_others_wrong.3: Secrets in config can leak; not recommended.

reference.title: Authenticate to Azure AI with managed identities

questions.question: You must log and review flagged prompts and completions for marketing. Which two signals are most relevant to capture for safety review?
options: Input and output hash with timestamps
questions.explanation: Capturing category/severity and hashed input/output with time helps audit and triage safety events.

reasoning.correct: Safety category/severity indicates risk; hashes allow correlation without storing PII.

why_others_wrong.0: Operational detail, not a safety signal.
why_others_wrong.2: Not sufficient to assess safety risk.
why_others_wrong.3: Useful, but without category/severity it lacks risk context.

questions.question: You need CI/CD for model training pipelines with lineage tracking. Which Azure service feature should you use?
options: Azure ML pipelines with MLflow tracking
questions.explanation: Azure ML pipelines orchestrate training and log metrics and artifacts via MLflow, enabling lineage.

reasoning.correct: Combines automation and tracking for reproducibility and governance.

why_others_wrong.0: Not reproducible or governed for CI/CD.
why_others_wrong.1: Lacks lineage and experiment tracking.
why_others_wrong.2: Not scalable or auditable.

reference.title: Track ML experiments in Azure Machine Learning

questions.question: To capture prompt/response latency and tokens used across prod, where should you centralize telemetry?
questions.explanation: Application Insights captures custom events/metrics for distributed tracing and cost monitoring.

reasoning.correct: Provides performance, usage, and cost-related telemetry for AI calls.

why_others_wrong.0: Provides recommendations, not tracing.
why_others_wrong.2: Enforces governance, not telemetry.
why_others_wrong.3: Reports service incidents, not app telemetry.

questions.question: You need PII detection and redaction before sending text to a model. Which capability should you use?
options: PII entity recognition in Language Studio
questions.explanation: PII entity recognition identifies and can mask PII prior to processing.

reasoning.correct: Pre-processing step ensures privacy compliance.

why_others_wrong.0: Classifies sentiment, not PII.
why_others_wrong.2: Focuses on healthcare PHI, not all PII.
why_others_wrong.3: Identifies key phrases, not PII.

options: Input and output hash with timestamps
questions.explanation: Capturing category/severity and hashed input/output with time helps audit and triage safety events.

reasoning.correct: Safety category/severity indicates risk; hashes allow correlation without storing PII.

why_others_wrong.0: Operational detail, not a safety signal.
why_others_wrong.1: Useful, but without category/severity it lacks risk context.
why_others_wrong.3: Not sufficient to assess safety risk.

questions.question: You need CI/CD for model training pipelines with lineage tracking. Which Azure service feature should you use?
options: Azure ML pipelines with MLflow tracking
questions.explanation: Azure ML pipelines orchestrate training and log metrics and artifacts via MLflow, enabling lineage.

reasoning.correct: Combines automation and tracking for reproducibility and governance.

why_others_wrong.0: Lacks lineage and experiment tracking.
why_others_wrong.2: Not scalable or auditable.
why_others_wrong.3: Not reproducible or governed for CI/CD.

reference.title: Track ML experiments in Azure Machine Learning

questions.question: To capture prompt/response latency and tokens used across dev, where should you centralize telemetry?
questions.explanation: Application Insights captures custom events/metrics for distributed tracing and cost monitoring.

reasoning.correct: Provides performance, usage, and cost-related telemetry for AI calls.

why_others_wrong.0: Reports service incidents, not app telemetry.
why_others_wrong.2: Provides recommendations, not tracing.
why_others_wrong.3: Enforces governance, not telemetry.

questions.question: You need PII detection and redaction before sending text to a model. Which capability should you use?
options: PII entity recognition in Language Studio
questions.explanation: PII entity recognition identifies and can mask PII prior to processing.

reasoning.correct: Pre-processing step ensures privacy compliance.

why_others_wrong.1: Focuses on healthcare PHI, not all PII.
why_others_wrong.2: Classifies sentiment, not PII.
why_others_wrong.3: Identifies key phrases, not PII.

questions.question: You need to prevent a generative model from returning unsafe outputs while building a chat app in East US. Which capability should you enable?
options: Content filtering with Azure AI Content Safety
questions.explanation: Azure AI Content Safety provides input and output filtering for harmful content. Tuning temperature or stop sequences does not guarantee safety.

reasoning.correct: Content Safety is designed to detect and block unsafe categories, aligning with Responsible AI requirements.

why_others_wrong.0: Reduces randomness but cannot enforce safety policies.
why_others_wrong.1: Stops generation at tokens but does not classify or block harmful content.
why_others_wrong.3: Controls throughput but not safety categories.

questions.question: You are designing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for app-engineering-1750. Which approaches improve grounding and reduce hallucinations? (Select two)
options: Add citations to the prompt format and require sources
Use embeddings with a vector store for semantic search
Disable system prompt to allow free-form answers
questions.explanation: Semantic retrieval with embeddings grounds the model in enterprise data; prompting for citations improves traceability.

reasoning.correct: RAG pairs relevant context with prompts; requiring citations encourages grounded outputs and improves reviewability.

why_others_wrong.2: Larger context without relevant sources does not ground answers.
why_others_wrong.3: Temperature > 1 increases randomness and does not ground answers.
why_others_wrong.4: Removes guardrails and increases hallucination likelihood.

questions.question: Your API app in West Europe must call Azure OpenAI without storing keys in code. What should you use?
options: Hard-coded API key in environment variable
questions.explanation: Managed identity enables Azure AD-based authentication without secrets in code.

reasoning.correct: Managed identities remove the need for key management and integrate with RBAC.

why_others_wrong.0: Environment variables are still secrets to rotate and protect.
why_others_wrong.2: Secrets in config can leak; not recommended.
why_others_wrong.3: Insecure and not supported.

questions.question: You need to prevent a generative model from returning unsafe outputs while building a chat app in East US. Which capability should you enable?

questions.question: You are designing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for app-engineering-1855. Which approaches improve grounding and reduce hallucinations? (Select two)
options: Disable system prompt to allow free-form answers
Add citations to the prompt format and require sources
Use embeddings with a vector store for semantic search
questions.explanation: Semantic retrieval with embeddings grounds the model in enterprise data; prompting for citations improves traceability.

reasoning.correct: RAG pairs relevant context with prompts; requiring citations encourages grounded outputs and improves reviewability.

why_others_wrong.0: Temperature > 1 increases randomness and does not ground answers.
why_others_wrong.1: Removes guardrails and increases hallucination likelihood.
why_others_wrong.3: Larger context without relevant sources does not ground answers.

why_others_wrong.3: Identifies key phrases, not PII.

questions.question: You need to prevent a generative model from returning unsafe outputs while building a chat app in East US. Which capability should you enable?
options: Content filtering with Azure AI Content Safety
questions.explanation: Azure AI Content Safety provides input and output filtering for harmful content. Tuning temperature or stop sequences does not guarantee safety.

reasoning.correct: Content Safety is designed to detect and block unsafe categories, aligning with Responsible AI requirements.

why_others_wrong.1: Reduces randomness but cannot enforce safety policies.
why_others_wrong.2: Stops generation at tokens but does not classify or block harmful content.
why_others_wrong.3: Controls throughput but not safety categories.

questions.question: You are designing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for app-engineering-1960. Which approaches improve grounding and reduce hallucinations? (Select two)
options: Use embeddings with a vector store for semantic search
Disable system prompt to allow free-form answers
Add citations to the prompt format and require sources
questions.explanation: Semantic retrieval with embeddings grounds the model in enterprise data; prompting for citations improves traceability.

reasoning.correct: RAG pairs relevant context with prompts; requiring citations encourages grounded outputs and improves reviewability.

why_others_wrong.1: Larger context without relevant sources does not ground answers.
why_others_wrong.2: Removes guardrails and increases hallucination likelihood.
why_others_wrong.3: Temperature > 1 increases randomness and does not ground answers.

questions.question: Your API app in West Europe must call Azure OpenAI without storing keys in code. What should you use?
options: Hard-coded API key in environment variable
questions.explanation: Managed identity enables Azure AD-based authentication without secrets in code.

reasoning.correct: Managed identities remove the need for key management and integrate with RBAC.

why_others_wrong.1: Insecure and not supported.
why_others_wrong.2: Environment variables are still secrets to rotate and protect.
why_others_wrong.3: Secrets in config can leak; not recommended.

reference.title: Authenticate to Azure AI with managed identities

questions.question: You must log and review flagged prompts and completions for engineering. Which two signals are most relevant to capture for safety review?
options: Input and output hash with timestamps
questions.explanation: Capturing category/severity and hashed input/output with time helps audit and triage safety events.

reasoning.correct: Safety category/severity indicates risk; hashes allow correlation without storing PII.

why_others_wrong.0: Useful, but without category/severity it lacks risk context.
why_others_wrong.1: Operational detail, not a safety signal.
why_others_wrong.3: Not sufficient to assess safety risk.

questions.question: You need CI/CD for model training pipelines with lineage tracking. Which Azure service feature should you use?
options: Azure ML pipelines with MLflow tracking
questions.explanation: Azure ML pipelines orchestrate training and log metrics and artifacts via MLflow, enabling lineage.

reasoning.correct: Combines automation and tracking for reproducibility and governance.

why_others_wrong.1: Lacks lineage and experiment tracking.
why_others_wrong.2: Not reproducible or governed for CI/CD.
why_others_wrong.3: Not scalable or auditable.

reference.title: Track ML experiments in Azure Machine Learning

questions.question: To capture prompt/response latency and tokens used across test, where should you centralize telemetry?
questions.explanation: Application Insights captures custom events/metrics for distributed tracing and cost monitoring.

reasoning.correct: Provides performance, usage, and cost-related telemetry for AI calls.

why_others_wrong.0: Enforces governance, not telemetry.
why_others_wrong.2: Reports service incidents, not app telemetry.
why_others_wrong.3: Provides recommendations, not tracing.

certificationInfo.title: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate
certificationInfo.description: This certification validates your expertise in building, managing, and deploying AI solutions that leverage Azure Cognitive Services, Azure Cognitive Search, and Microsoft Bot Framework.
prerequisites: Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) or equivalent knowledge
Experience with Azure services and workloads
Knowledge of programming languages (C#, Python, JavaScript)
Understanding of machine learning concepts and algorithms

skillsMeasured.category: Plan and manage Azure Cognitive Services (15-20%)
skills: Plan and configure Azure Cognitive Services
Plan and configure Azure Cognitive Services pricing tiers
Plan and configure Azure Cognitive Services authentication
Plan and configure Azure Cognitive Services networking
Plan and configure Azure Cognitive Services monitoring
